title: Acropora DNA Extraction Using Qiagen DNeasy Tissue Kit
author: Iliana Baums
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bgjqjumw

steps.title: Use sterile technique to cut 3-4...
steps.instruction: Use sterile technique to cut 3-4 polyps (skeleton with tissue) off the coral fragment with a razor blade.

steps.title: Transfer the pieces to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the pieces to a 1.5 ml tube/96-well plate.

steps.title: Add 180 ul of Buffer ATL...
steps.instruction: Add 180 ul of Buffer ATL and 20 ul of proteinase K (from Qiagen kit) to each tube/well.

steps.title: If using the tubes, invert the...
steps.instruction: If using the tubes, invert the tubes several times to gently mix the reagents. *Do not vortex*

steps.title: a
steps.instruction: a.The mixture will have a slight yellow hue after lysis from the algal symbionts.

steps.title: b
steps.instruction: b.The tubes or plate can be placed on a rocking platform for gentle agitation.

steps.title: Follow Qiagen’s protocol with the following changes
steps.instruction: Follow Qiagen’s protocol with the following changes: Following step 6 of the spin-column protocol, we perform the optional centrifugation for 1 min at 20,000 xg to dry the column membrane.

steps.title: Elution 1- Add 100 ul of...
steps.instruction: Elution 1- Add 100 ul of low TE buffer directly to the column membrane, incubate for 1 min and centrifuge as recommended. a.This elution typically contains smaller fragmented DNA.

steps.title: Elution 2 – Move the column to a new 1
steps.instruction: Elution 2 – Move the column to a new 1.5 ml tube. Repeat elution as above, but increase incubation of 100 ul of low TE to 5 min. a.This elution contains high molecular weight DNA and is used for downstream analyses. b.Store at -20 °C.

steps.title: Run DNA on a 1
steps.instruction: Run DNA on a 1.5% agarose gel to check extraction quality of elution 1 and 2. Look for one high molecular weight band at the top of the gel with little smearing.
